channel.title: SSbreakingHeadlines
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbreakingHeadlines, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: US may indict former Cuban president Raul Castro in coming days: Sources
item.description: The U.S. could announce a criminal indictment of former Cuban president Raul Castro soon, sources familiar with the matter said.

item.title: Long Island Rail Road workers strike after contract negotiations fail
item.description: The strike, the first for the LIRR since 1994, began after the MTA and unions representing 3,500 workers could not reach an agreement.

item.title: Murdaugh murder retrial: Prosecutors say death penalty is possible
item.description: The South Carolina Attorney General indicated that prosecutors may seek the death penalty in Alex Murdaugh's retrial.

item.title: DOJ plans 'Truth and Justice Commission' to compensate allies: Sources
item.description: The DOJ is finalizing a deal to create a commission that would pay claims from alleged victims of government weaponization.
